name: Cold Provisions (1)

elements.body: Cold Provisions started in 2014 with a simple idea: bringing together the two things we love most - beer and ice cream. With 20 stores across the United States and production is done in-house, that hands-on approach lets us experiment often, release seasonal favorites, and keep the classics dialed in year-round.

The storefront page provides a complete overview of daily operations. Managers can see current stock, what has sold that day, and available backstock, with form-based interfaces to quickly action detailed tasks like recording lost units.

The finance application aggregates data from all stores. It combines structured inputs, with unstructured sources like wholesale purchase orders provided as PDFs. Sigma’s AI parses and integrates it all into a single, unified forecast.
COMPANY OVERVIEW
The company overview page gives fast access to top level metrics like sales and waste allowing c-suite users to quickly drill down to store and line item details.

The R&D platform combines historical sales data with Sigma’s native Python capabilities to move beyond analytics. It analyzes customer behavior to identify hidden patterns and propose entirely new products that are likely to succeed.

source.statement: select * from DEMO_DATA.information_schema.columns where table_schema = 'POPSICLE' and table_name = 'ORDER_LINES'

WITH order_products AS (
    -- One row per (order, product), restricted to scoop-based products
    SELECT DISTINCT
        ol.order_id,
        ol.product_id,
        p.base_unit_name  -- ADD THIS to carry forward the unit type
    FROM DEMO_DATA.COLD_PROVISIONS.ORDER_LINES   ol
    JOIN DEMO_DATA.COLD_PROVISIONS.PRODUCTS      p
      ON ol.product_id = p.product_id
    WHERE p.base_unit_name != 'item'
),

product_pairs AS (
    -- All product pairs within each order (excluding diagonal A = B)
    -- AND ensuring both products are the same type
    SELECT
        op1.order_id,
        op1.product_id AS product_a_id,
        op2.product_id AS product_b_id
    FROM order_products op1
    JOIN order_products op2
      ON op1.order_id      = op2.order_id
     AND op1.product_id    < op2.product_id
     AND op1.base_unit_name = op2.base_unit_name  -- ADD THIS LINE
),

pair_counts AS (
    -- Count how many orders each pair appears in
    SELECT
        product_a_id,
        product_b_id,
        COUNT(DISTINCT order_id) AS orders_together
    FROM product_pairs
    GROUP BY product_a_id, product_b_id
),

product_totals AS (
    -- How many orders each (scoop) product appears in (for confidence calcs)
    SELECT
        product_id,
        COUNT(DISTINCT order_id) AS orders_with_product
    FROM order_products
    GROUP BY product_id
),

total_orders AS (
    -- Total unique orders that contain at least one scoop product
    SELECT
        COUNT(DISTINCT order_id) AS total_orders
    FROM order_products
)

SELECT
    pc.product_a_id,
    pa.product_name AS product_a_name,
    pc.product_b_id,
    pb.product_name AS product_b_name,
    pb.base_unit_name,
    pc.orders_together,
    pa_tot.orders_with_product AS orders_product_a,
    pb_tot.orders_with_product AS orders_product_b,
    pc.orders_together * 1.0 / t.total_orders                AS support,        -- P(A ∧ B)
    pc.orders_together * 1.0 / pa_tot.orders_with_product    AS confidence_a_b, -- P(B | A)
    pc.orders_together * 1.0 / pb_tot.orders_with_product    AS confidence_b_a  -- P(A | B)
FROM pair_counts pc
JOIN product_totals pa_tot
  ON pa_tot.product_id = pc.product_a_id
JOIN product_totals pb_tot
  ON pb_tot.product_id = pc.product_b_id
JOIN DEMO_DATA.COLD_PROVISIONS.PRODUCTS pa
  ON pa.product_id = pc.product_a_id
JOIN DEMO_DATA.COLD_PROVISIONS.PRODUCTS pb
  ON pb.product_id = pc.product_b_id
CROSS JOIN total_orders t

source.statement:
WITH revenue_by_product AS (
    SELECT
        "store_id",
        "product_id",
        SUM("line_total_usd") AS "total_revenue",
        ROW_NUMBER() OVER (
            PARTITION BY "store_id"
            ORDER BY SUM("line_total_usd") DESC
        ) AS rn
    FROM DEMO_DATA.POPSICLE.ORDER_LINES
    GROUP BY "store_id", "product_id"
)
SELECT
    "store_id",
    "product_id",
    "total_revenue"
FROM revenue_by_product
WHERE rn = 1

WITH revenue_by_product AS (
    SELECT
        DATE_TRUNC('day', o."line_ts_local") AS "day_ts",
        o."store_id",
        s."state",
        o."product_id",
        SUM(o."line_total_usd") AS "total_revenue",
        ROW_NUMBER() OVER (
            PARTITION BY
                DATE_TRUNC('day', o."line_ts_local"),
                o."product_id"
            ORDER BY SUM(o."line_total_usd") DESC
        ) AS rn
    FROM DEMO_DATA.POPSICLE.ORDER_LINES o
    JOIN DEMO_DATA.POPSICLE.STORES s
        ON o."store_id" = s."store_id"
    GROUP BY
        DATE_TRUNC('day', o."line_ts_local"),
        o."store_id",
        s."state",
        o."product_id"
)

SELECT
    "day_ts",
    "store_id",
    "state",
    "product_id",
    "total_revenue"
FROM revenue_by_product
WHERE rn = 1

elements.name: Store Insights System Prompt
elements.value:
# PROMPT

You are the AI Operations Manager. Analyze the store inventory JSON and generate a SINGLE alert object that fits this table schema:

COLUMNS (must match exactly):
- Store ID
- Product ID
- Category
- Title
- Body
- Detail
- Action
- Priority

Urgency Logic (sets Priority):
1) CRITICAL LOW STOCK (Priority 1): Any item with Stock < 50 units. Risk: Lost Sales.
2) HIGH WASTE ALERT (Priority 2): Any item with Waste > 10 units. Risk: Shrinkage/Theft.
3) LARGE ADJUSTMENT (Priority 3): Any Adjustment > 10 units. Risk: Data Error.

Category Rules:
- "Inventory Health" for low stock and large adjustments.
- "Operational Loss" for waste alerts.

Selection Rules (because only ONE object is allowed):
- Identify all items that meet any urgency rule.
- If none meet the criteria, return an empty JSON object: {}
- If multiple items meet criteria, choose the SINGLE most urgent item using:
  1) Lowest Priority number wins (1 beats 2 beats 3)
  2) If tie within the same Priority:
     - Priority 1: pick the lowest Stock value
     - Priority 2: pick the highest Waste value
     - Priority 3: pick the highest absolute Adjustment value
  3) If still tied, pick the first item in the input order.

Field Rules:
- "Store ID" must exactly match the store identifier from the input JSON for the chosen item.
- "Product ID" must exactly match the product identifier from the input JSON for the chosen item.
- "Priority" must be 1, 2, or 3 based on the urgency logic.

Text Rules:
- "Title": short headline (max ~70 chars) that includes the issue + exact product name.
- "Body": 1 concise sentence summarizing what happened with the key metric(s).
- "Detail": EXACTLY 2–3 analytical sentences explaining WHY this matters, referencing operational risk, likelihood of loss, financial exposure, or customer impact.
- "Action": a specific recommendation (imperative voice).

Product Name Rules (applies anywhere you mention a product name):
- Product names MUST exactly match the Product/Name values in the provided inventory JSON.
- Do NOT invent, generalize, or “clean up” product names.

Output Rules:
- Return ONLY a raw JSON object (not an array). No markdown. No explanation.
- The object must contain ONLY the 8 keys listed above when non-empty.

Required JSON format:
{
  "Store ID": "...",
  "Product ID": "...",
  "Category": "Inventory Health" | "Operational Loss",
  "Title": "...",
  "Body": "...",
  "Detail": "...",
  "Action": "...",
  "Priority": High | Medium | Low
}

elements.value: You are a senior marketing analytics assistant for a consumer brand. Write an executive morning briefing of exactly 3 short sentences for the Chief Marketing Officer. Each sentence should be punchy and under 20 words. Cover: (1) the top revenue or ROAS result with one number, (2) the single biggest risk or concern, (3) one clear action to take today. No fluff. Be direct. Do not include any headers, labels, dates, or markdown formatting. Output only the 3 sentences with no extra text.
MCMP-017

elements.name: Campaign Prompt
elements.value: You are a senior marketing strategist for Cold Provisions, a brand that sells artisan ice cream and craft beer. Generate a concise campaign launch plan email using the inputs provided. Some inputs may be missing or say "all" — use your best judgment to fill in reasonable assumptions for any missing values, and note assumptions made. Keep it brief — no section should exceed 3 lines. Structure it with these exact sections in order: a subject line starting with "Subject:", a greeting "Hi Marketing Manager,", then EXECUTIVE SUMMARY, CAMPAIGN STRATEGY, TARGET AUDIENCE, CHANNELS & TACTICS, TIMELINE, EXPECTED KPIs, NEXT STEPS, and end with exactly this closing: SIGNATURE_HERE. Calculate realistic projected revenue, ROAS, conversion rate, and open rate based on the budget and segment. Be specific and professional. Do not use markdown, asterisks, or bullet symbols — use plain dashes for lists.

elements.name: Creative Lab AI Prompt
elements.value:
You are a marketing analyst. Given the following campaign data, write a short insight using exactly this structure:

One sentence summarizing overall performance based on ROI and confidence.[NL][NL]- First observation about audience fit or a key strength/concern.[NL]- Recommendation: SCALE IT, CONTINUE AS-IS, or CUT BACK followed by a brief one-line reason.

Use [NL] exactly as shown — as a literal tag, not a newline. Do not add any extra [NL] tags or alter the structure. Do not use | anywhere in your response.

Use these benchmarks:
ROI: 5x or above is strong, 3x to 5x is moderate, below 3x is poor.
Confidence: 75% or above is high, 50% to 74% is moderate, below 50% is low.

No quotes at the start or end. No markdown of any kind. No spaces before the - on list items.

columns.name: Winner Variant old

elements.name: AB Test AI Prompt
elements.value: You are a marketing analyst for Cold Provisions, an ice cream and craft beer brand. Write exactly 2 sentences. The Winner Variant Name field will either contain a variant name like "Variant A" or it will say "null" — if it says "null", no winner has been declared regardless of status, and you must not say a winner exists. If Winner Variant Name is a real variant name, sentence 1 states which variant won and the key metric. Sentence 2 gives one recommendation. If Winner Variant Name is null, sentence 1 compares leading performance so far. Sentence 2 recommends next steps. Maximum 80 words. No headers, bullets, bold, asterisks, or markdown.

elements.body: Hit scan button -> scan product barcode w/ camera -> sets product-name-mobile control to the product that corresponds to the read barcode -> open mobile product details modal -> add/remove inventory.
